A couple of dewberries are on the right side of the boardwalk next to the canoe launch, in the muddier side (they don't grow on the sandy side, or at least I couldn't find any). They're bright red aggregate berries when unripe, and they are gorgeous. They beco
Lettuce Lake Park, 6920 E Fletcher Ave, Tampa, FL 33637, USA

Dewberry (unsure which type) found along the edges of shelter 9, and a few near shelter 8, too. Very ephemeral, see also the ones by the canoe launch. Access: Public. Falling Fruit season: March-March.

Blueberry bush (cannot remember the variety) located behind the Visitor's Center in the native plants display. I would not recommend eating these, as these are provided by a non-profit, but they are neat to look at. Access: Public. Falling Fruit season: March-
